headline: JPMorgan CIO: Coach AI Cuts Info Retrieval 95%, Links to 20% Sales Growth
the_so_what: A 95% reduction in information retrieval time correlating with 20% gross sales growth across 4,000 private bank advisors is the most concrete published evidence that advisor AI deployments translate to revenue, not just efficiency. Morgan Stanley reports 98% adoption of its AI tools but has not disclosed a revenue impact. BofA claims up to 4 hours saved per meeting but without sales conversion data. JPMorgan is the first to connect the dots between AI productivity and top-line growth — a benchmark that peers will now be expected to match.
company_name: JPMorgan Private Bank and Wealth Management
source_name: Reuters — JPMorgan says AI helped boost sales, add clients in market turmoil
summary: In a Reuters interview published May 5, 2025, JPMorgan Chase's AWM CIO Mike Urciuoli stated: 'Our advisors are finding the right information up to 95% faster — which means they spend less time searching and more time engaging in meaningful conversations with clients.' The tool — called Coach AI in wealth management and Connect Coach in the Private Bank — is deployed across approximately 4,000 advisers serving JPMorgan's high-net-worth private bank clients. JPMorgan stated the Asset & Wealth Management division saw a 20% year-over-year increase in gross sales between 2023 and 2024, citing AI-driven tools as enabling teams to 'focus more effectively on high-impact client work.' The bank also disclosed that its GenAI toolkit is deployed on the desktops of more than 200,000 employees, more than half of whom use it several times a day, and that AI has saved approximately $1.5 billion across the firm through fraud prevention, personalisation, trading, and operational efficiencies. Looking ahead, JPMorgan stated Coach AI will help advisers expand their client rosters by 50% over the next three to five years. Harvard Business School published a case study on JPMorgan's AI programme, concluding: 'JPMorgan is playing an entirely different game.'

key_stat.label: Gross sales growth YoY (2023–2024) — JPMorgan Asset & Wealth Management, AI tools cited as key driver (Reuters, May 2025)

verified_claims: Mike Urciuoli (CIO, JPMorgan AWM): 'advisors are finding the right information up to 95% faster' — verbatim quote, Reuters May 5, 2025
20% year-over-year increase in gross sales 2023–2024 — attributed to JPMorgan as company statement in Reuters article
Coach AI deployed to approximately 4,000 advisers serving high-net-worth private bank clients — Reuters, citing HBS case study context
GenAI toolkit on desktops of 200,000+ employees, more than half use several times a day — Reuters, May 5, 2025
$1.5 billion saved across firm via AI in fraud prevention, personalisation, trading, operations — Reuters, May 5, 2025
50% more clients per adviser target over 3–5 years — Reuters, attributed to JPMorgan
Harvard Business School case study on JPMorgan GenAI confirmed — Reuters, May 5, 2025
unverified_claims: 20% gross sales growth not separately verified in Investor Day deck — appears only in Reuters article attributed to JPMorgan as a company (not a named executive quote)
_governance.notes: All claims sourced from Reuters article by Nupur Anand, May 5, 2025. Named source: Mike Urciuoli (CIO, JPMorgan AWM) for the 95% figure. The 20% gross sales figure is attributed to JPMorgan in the Reuters article but does not appear in the 2025 Investor Day AWM deck. human_approved: Track 2 entry, all claims read directly from Reuters source.

landscape_context.peer_comparison: Morgan Stanley (scaled, 98% adoption), BofA (scaled, 30B interactions), Goldman (deployed, 46K employees)
